provenance.source_corpus: Long-term recovery of epiphytic communities in the Great Bear Rainforest of coastal British Columbia

source_spans.text: Oldgrowth trees supported about twice as many epiphyte species as young or mature trees in second growth stands

Oldgrowth communities separated from young and mature communities

there was no indication that these residual trees (measured at the stand-level) affected epiphyte community composition in regenerating trees
